title: Review: wallet/src/*.rs security/correctness round 14
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — scan_block in-memory state diverges from DB on save failure (nie-en5f.6) is the most dangerous issue: it silently corrupts the commitment tree on restart and makes all subsequent note proofs invalid until a full rescan. The tree-full continuation (nie-en5f.7) is theoretical at current chain size. Spent-witness accumulation (nie-en5f.2) affects performance over time. The Zip212Enforcement issue (nie-en5f.4) is a protocol compliance gap with no practical exploit path. Documentation/diagnostic issues are minor.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: did not trace all callers of WalletStore outside the wallet crate, could not run the full test suite, and scanner state machine restart interactions are complex. Would raise confidence with: integration test that injects a save_block_state failure mid-block and verifies subsequent scan consistency.
